area_anchor: Brikette — Inbox analytics show incorrect numbers and have a security gap in how queries are built
current_truth: Volume metrics double-count threads that have multiple events of the same type. Quality metrics join drafts to events without matching time periods. The days parameter is interpolated directly into SQL strings instead of using parameterized queries. No rate limit or max days cap exists on the endpoint.
next_scope_now: Fix volume query to deduplicate per thread, constrain quality join time periods, parameterize SQL days value, add max days cap of 365.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: volume metrics double-count; quality join unconstrained; SQL interpolation; no rate limit
why: The inbox analytics dashboard shows inflated numbers because it counts the same email thread multiple times when retries or recovery happen. The SQL queries also have a security gap where user input is put directly into the query text instead of using safe parameters. Someone could also request years of data in one call and slow down the whole system.

intended_outcome.statement: Analytics queries produce accurate deduplicated metrics, use parameterized SQL, and enforce a maximum time window.
